title: antirrhinum sempervirens subsp. microphyllum
intro: Antirrhinum sempervirens subsp. microphyllum, also known as the dwarf snapdragon or small-leaved snapdragon, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Antirrhinum sempervirens subsp. microphyllum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and applications.
